debugId.description: Unique identifier for the instance of this error

errorCode.description: A unique machine-friendly, but human-readable identifier for the error

createdAt.description: The time at which this asynchronous operation is submitted.

endedAt.description: Time at which the operation completed (entered a `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, or `CANCELLED` state).

id.description: Primary identifier for the asynchronous operation given by the system.

oneOf.description: A list of progress update objects, which may be empty.

progressPercent.description: Percent progress of the operation as an integer value of 0 to 100.

oneOf.description: List of references to resources (other than the source resource) which were created, updated, or deleted during the operation.

sourceResourceUri.description: URI reference to the resource that initiated the operation.

startedAt.description: Time at which the operation entered the `RUNNING` state.

suggestedPollingIntervalSeconds.description: Number of seconds recommended for clients to poll for updates.

timeoutMinutes.description: Number of minutes after the last update before the operation moves into the `TIMEDOUT` state.

updatedAt.description: The time at which this asynchronous operation is last updated.

id.description: Unique ID of the the wellness event

next.description: The asynchronous operation ID acts as the pagination cursor for the next page of resources. Empty string when no more pages exist.

total.description: Total number of items (asynchronous operation) for the current filter criteria.

next.description: The event ID acts as the pagination cursor for the next page of resources. Empty string when no more pages exist.

total.description: Total number of items (wellness events) for the current filter criteria.

next.description: Support case ID, which acts as the pagination cursor for the next page of support cases. Empty string when no more pages exist.

total.description: Total number of items (support cases) for the current filter criteria.

ManualCaseDetails.description: Curated details for manually created support cases. Only present when `source` is `manual`.

accountName.description: Account name associated with the case.

alternateContact.description: Alternate contact for the case. Only present when an alternate contact was provided during case creation.

name.description: The full name of the contact person.

phone.description: The phone number of the contact.

preferredContactMethod.description: Specifies how the contact prefers to be reached. Contains the label and type.

type.description: The contact method type, for example, "email" or "phone".

category.description: The primary help category, for example, "Backup and Recovery".

hasAttachments.description: Indicates whether the case includes file attachments. Set to `true` if one or more files are attached.

orderNumber.description: Purchase order number associated with the device or subscription.

outage.description: A boolean that declares whether an outage has occurred.

name.description: The full name of the contact person.

phone.description: The phone number of the contact.

preferredContactMethod.description: Specifies how the contact prefers to be reached. Contains the label and type.

type.description: The contact method type, for example, "email" or "phone".

label.description: The human-readable priority label, for example, "Critical".

serialNumber.description: Serial number of the device associated with the case.

subcategory.description: The sub-category selection, displayed as a breadcrumb when multiple sub-categories apply. For example, "Disaster Recovery > Data Protection".

subscriptionKey.description: License key that activates the device subscription.

archive.description: Boolean indicating whether the wellness event is archived or not.

flag.description: Boolean indicating whether the wellness event is flagged or not.

read.description: Boolean indicating the read status of the wellness event.

success.description: Indicates whether the event identified by the ID was updated or not.

ServiceManagerRef.description: Reference to the service manager (application) of the device that generated this wellness event.

id.description: Unique ID of the service manager (application) of the device that generated this wellness event.

resourceUri.description: URI to the service manager (application) of the device that generated this wellness event.

archive.description: Specifies if the wellness event has been archived or not.

causes.description: A list of possible root causes for the event condition.

correlated.description: Specifies if this wellness event is related to another wellness event or not.

flag.description: Specifies if the wellness event has been flagged or not.

id.description: Primary identifier for the wellness event given by the system.

isFlex.description: Specifies if the device associated with this wellness event is a Flex device.

productName.description: The name of the product to which this event belongs.

read.description: Specifies if the wellness event has been read or not.

region.description: The region of the device to which the wellness event belongs.

resourceUri.description: URI to the wellness event itself (a self link).

serviceName.description: The name of the service to which this event belongs.

source.description: Indicates how the event was created.

supportCaseCreationOpUri.description: URI to get details of the support case creation operation.

title.description: Short description of the wellness event.

updatedAt.description: The last date and time the wellness event was updated.

asset.description: The device the wellness event belongs to.

name.description: The serial number or name of the asset.

serialNumber.description: The serial number of the asset.

asyncOpLogMessage.description: Progress update object of an asynchronous operation.

message.description: Description of a recorded state of an asynchronous operation.

timestamp.description: The time at which the state of asynchronous operation is recorded.

asyncOpResult.description: Resource that is newly created by the asynchronous operation.

resourceUri.description: Reference to the resource that is newly created by the asynchronous operation

caseHistoryData.description: History of the actions performed on the support case object.

action.description: The type of case update, for example, `Case Opened` and `Updated`.

timeStamp.description: Date and time representing when the support case update occurred.

cause.description: Short explanation about potential cause for occurrence of this wellness event.

recommendation.description: A recommended action to resolve the wellness condition.

title.description: One sentence summary of potential cause.

visibility.description: A visibility filter. Null or absent implies universally visible.

condition.description: Specifies details about the nature of the wellness event.

category.description: The category the condition belongs to.

name.description: Human-readable name given to the condition.

severity.description: The severity level of the wellness event.

content.description: A descriptive explanation of the wellness event.

mediaType.description: Specifies the media type of the content.

status.description: Current status of the wellness event.

correlatedTo.description: List of wellness events correlated to this wellness event.

currentState.description: The current status. It is the most recent computed state for the event.

caseNumber.description: The case number assigned to the support case.

createdAt.description: The date and time of support case creation.

crm.description: The customer relationship management (CRM) system used to create the support case.

generation.description: Monotonically increasing update counter to track the support case version.

source.description: Indicates how the support case was created.

status.description: The current status of the support case.

updatedAt.description: The last date and time the support case was updated.

user.description: The first and last name of the user that escalated the wellness event. Only relevant to manually escalated cases.

Bearer.description: Personal access token compliant with RFC8725 issued by the HPE GreenLake platform.

info.description: The HPE GreenLake for Wellness APIs facilitates the automation of IT operations by enabling you to monitor wellness events related to your infrastructure. To further streamline integration workflows, these APIs provide various filtering options and KPI metrics.
info.title: HPE GreenLake for Wellness

get.description:
Retrieves a list of all asynchronous operations organized in descending order based on their creation time. This ensures the most recent asynchronous operations are presented first.

__Pagination__: This endpoint exclusively supports cursor-based pagination.

__Filtering__: The following are the supported filter parameters: * `state`— The `state` filter parameter only supports the `eq` operator and its value should be a valid string. * `createdAt` * `updatedAt` * `endedAt` * `startedAt`

parameters.description: The `filter` query parameter is used to filter a set of resources. The returned set of resources matches the criteria in the filter query parameter. The value of the filter query parameter is a subset of [OData V4](https://www.odata.org/documentation/) filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators.

aOp-btw-time-range.summary: Retrieve a list of asynchronous operations initiated between the given timestamps

aOp-start-lt.summary: Retrieve a list of asynchronous operations that went into the `RUNNING` state after the given timestamp

aOp-timestamp-gt.summary: Retrieve a list of asynchronous operations initiated after the given timestamp

aOp-timestamp-lt.summary: Retrieve a list of asynchronous operations initiated before the given timestamp

state.summary: Retrieve a list of asynchronous operations that are in the given `state`

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Specifies the number of asynchronous operations to be returned.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Specifies the ID, which acts as the pagination cursor for the next page of asynchronous operations.
parameters.name: next

value.message: Only total is supported for select query param

get.description:
Retrieves a list of wellness events, in descending order of creation time so that the most recent events are listed first.

__Pagination__— This endpoint exclusively supports cursor-based pagination, using the `next` query parameter.

__Filtering__— The following are the supported filter parameters: * `condition/severity` * `status/currentState` * `supportCase/caseNumber` * `asset/serialNumber` * `productName` * `serviceName` * `region` * `serviceManager/id ` * `source` * `isFlex` * `flag` * `archive` * `read` * `createdAt` * `updatedAt`

parameters.description:
The `filter` query parameter is used to filter a set of resources. The returned set of resources matches the criteria in the filter query parameter. The value of the filter query parameter is a subset of [OData V4](https://www.odata.org/documentation/) filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators.

| Class | Supported |
|---|---|
| Types | string, boolean, timestamp |
| Comparison | `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `ge`, `lt`, `le`, `in` |
| Logical Expressions | `and` |
| Functions | `contains()`, `startswith()`, `endswith()` |

The following examples are not an exhaustive list of all possible filtering options.

archive.description: Retrieve events that are not archived.

btw-time-range.description: Retrieve events created between the given timestamps.

contains.description: Retrieve events that contains the given number in the support case number.
contains.summary: Filter with case number of the support case

isFlex.description: Retrieve events that belong to Flex devices.

isFlex-false.description: Retrieve events that do not belong to Flex devices.

lt-time-range.description: Retrieve events created before the given timestamp.

product.description: Retrieve events that belong to productName 'HPE Alletra 9000'.

region.description: Retrieve events that belong to devices in the given region.

serialNumber.description: Retrieve events that belong to the specified serial number.

service.description: Retrieve events that belong to specified services.

service-manager.description: Retrieve events that belong to specified Service Manager.

service-manager-region.description: Retrieve events that belong to specified Service Manager and a Region.
service-manager-region.summary: Filter with Service Manager and Region

severity.summary: Filter with the severity of the condition

severity-product.description: Retrieve events with severity 'warning' and that belong to productName 'HPE Alletra 9000'.
severity-product.summary: Filter with the severity of the condition and product
severity-product.value: condition/severity eq 'warning' and productName eq 'HPE Alletra 9000'

source.description: Retrieve only auto-generated (device) events, excluding manually created cases.

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description:
Searches for wellness events that contain the given search string. A search string can include alphanumeric characters, a space character (Unicode `U+0020`) or a hyphen (-). Apart from space characters and hyphens, no other special characters are supported. Including an unsupported character might cause inaccurate results. The minimum length of a search string is 2 characters and maximum is 100 characters. When performing a search, it's important to use specific terms. A generic search term may cause the search to timeout. * `title` * `condition.category` * `condition.name` * `condition.severity` * `asset.name` * `asset.product` * `asset.serialNumber` * `status.currentStatus` * `supportCase.casenumber` * `supportCase.casestatus` * `serviceName` * `productName`

__Note:__ You can use both the `filter` and `text-search` parameters in the same query. If both are provided, the filter is applied first, and then text-search is performed on the filtered results.

temperature.summary: Search for wellness events that contain the string `temperature`

parameters.name: text-search

parameters.description:
The `select` query parameter is used to limit the properties returned for support cases. The value of the `select` query parameter is a comma separated list of properties. All properties are returned if the select parameter is omitted.

__Note:__ Only the `total` property is supported.

total.description: Retrieve the total count of events for the current filter.

parameters.description: The `next` parameter represents the ID of an event used as a pagination cursor to retrieve the next set of wellness events. The parameter must be a valid UUID and be a part of the response of the previous request.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of support cases associated with wellness events. The results are in descending order of creation time, so that the most recent support cases are listed first.

__Pagination__: This endpoint exclusively supports cursor-based pagination, using the `next` query parameter.

__Filtering__: The following are the supported filter parameters: **Note:** Using any `details/*` filter implicitly limits results to manual cases (`source` = `manual`). * `event/id` — The `event/id` filter parameter only supports the `eq` operator and its value should be a valid wellness event ID. * `caseNumber` * `status` * `createdAt` — Timestamp when the support case was created. * `updatedAt` — Timestamp when the support case was last updated. * `source` — How the case was created. Only supports the `eq` operator. Values: `manual`, `auto`. * `details/category` — Primary help category assigned to the case. Only applicable to manual cases. * `details/serialNumber` — Serial number of the device associated with the case. Only applicable to manual cases. * `details/subscriptionKey` — License key for the subscription associated with the case. Only applicable to manual cases. * `details/primaryContact/email` — Email address of the primary contact for the case. Only applicable to manual cases. * `details/alternateContact/email` — Email address of an alternate contact for the case. Only applicable to manual cases. * `event/condition/severity` * `event/status/currentState` * `event/productName` * `event/serviceName` * `event/createdAt` — Timestamp when the underlying wellness event was created. * `event/updatedAt` — Timestamp when the underlying wellness event was last updated. * `event/isFlex` — Whether the device associated with the event is a Flex device. Only supports the `eq` operator. Values: `true`, `false`.

parameters.description:
The `filter` query parameter is used to filter a set of resources. The returned set of resources matches the criteria in the filter query parameter. The value of the filter query parameter is a subset of [OData V4](https://www.odata.org/documentation/) filter expressions consisting of simple comparison operations joined by logical operators.

| Class | Supported |
|---|---|
| Types | string, timestamp, guid |
| Comparison | `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `ge`, `lt`, `le`, `in` |
| Logical Expressions | `and` |
| Functions | `contains()`, `startswith()`, `endswith()` |

The following examples are not an exhaustive list of all possible filtering options.

btw-time-range.summary: Retrieve a list of support cases created between given timestamps

details-alternate-contact-email.summary: Retrieve manual cases by alternate contact email

details-category.summary: Retrieve manual cases for a specific help category

details-primary-contact-email.summary: Retrieve manual cases by primary contact email

details-serial-number.summary: Retrieve manual cases for a specific device serial number

details-subscription-key.summary: Retrieve manual cases for a specific subscription key

event-id.summary: Retrieve the support case details associated with the given wellness event

event-isFlex.description: Retrieve support cases associated with Flex device events.
event-isFlex.summary: Retrieve support cases for Flex devices

event-isFlex-false.description: Retrieve support cases associated with non-Flex device events.
event-isFlex-false.summary: Retrieve support cases for non-Flex devices

event-timestamp-gt.summary: Retrieve a list of support cases for wellness events created after the given timestamp

event-timestamp-lt.summary: Retrieve a list of support cases for wellness events created before the given timestamp

lt-time-range.summary: Retrieve a list of support cases created before the given timestamp

source-manual.summary: Retrieve only manually created support cases

parameters.name: filter

__Note:__ Only the `total` property is supported.

tags.description:
Wellness events provide health insights about your HPE products and services and show information about automatically created support cases. Wellness events allow you to:
  • Identify potential vulnerabilities before they affect your environment.
  • Proactively monitor the health of your HPE products and services.
  • View AI-powered recommendations about events.
tags.name: Events

tags.description: Create and retrieve support cases associated with wellness events.
tags.name: Support Cases

tags.description:
Asynchronous APIs are APIs that do not return data immediately. This means you can make multiple requests at once. A high-level overview of the asynchronous API process:
  1. You make a request to an asynchronous API endpoint.
  2. HPE GreenLake returns an acknowledgment, typically a `202 Accepted`, and provides a URI to the async-operation resource in the `Location` header.
  3. HPE GreenLake continues to process the request.
  4. When completed, HPE GreenLake returns the requested information.
tags.name: Async Operations
